lessons.title: Preparation of Parts and Adequate Lighting
objectives: Prepare part surfaces correctly, recognizing contaminants that block penetrant entry
Verify adequate white light or UV-A lighting before processing and inspection

lessons.title: Penetrant Application and Dwell
objectives: Apply penetrant by spray, brush, and immersion while maintaining surface coverage
Select appropriate dwell times based on material, temperature, and suspected discontinuity type

lessons.title: Removal of Surface Penetrant
objectives: Remove excess surface penetrant by water-wash, solvent-wipe, and emulsifier techniques
Recognize and avoid overwashing and underwashing conditions

lessons.title: Guided Practice: Preparation, Application, and Removal
objectives: Execute the preparation, application, and removal steps in sequence on practice scenarios
Identify processing errors and their effect on indication quality
cp105Topics: 2.3 Application of penetrant to parts (applied practice)
